message:
🌱 Add harbor: LDAP/OIDC group member role resolution mission (#894)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,74 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "harbor-19928-with-the-ldap-auth-or-oidc-auth-what-if-the-current-user-is-a-group",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "harbor: With the LDAP_AUTH or OIDC_AUTH, what if the current user is a group member and the group is granted as admin role?",
+    "description": "With the LDAP_AUTH or OIDC_AUTH, what if the current user is a group member and the group is granted as admin role?. This issue affects 9+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify harbor troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your harbor deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n harbor --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review harbor configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant harbor configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor\nkubectl get configmap -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=harbor\n```\nWith the LDAP_AUTH or OIDC_AUTH, what if the current user is a group member and the group is granted as admin role?\n\n_Originally posted by  in https://github.com/goharbor/harbor/pull/19543#discussion_r1385879526_\n       \nWe ran into this issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade Harbor to fix group-based role resolution",
+        "description": "Upgrade Harbor to a version that correctly resolves group-based project roles:\n```bash\n# Update the Harbor Helm repo\nhelm repo update harbor\n\n# Upgrade Harbor\nhelm upgrade harbor harbor/harbor -n harbor -f values.yaml\n\n# Verify pods restart successfully\nkubectl rollout status deployment/harbor-core -n harbor\n```\nAfter upgrading, users who belong to LDAP/OIDC groups with project admin roles will have full admin permissions on those projects."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm With the LDAP_AUTH or OIDC_AUTH, what if the\u2026 is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n harbor --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n harbor --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "When using LDAP or OIDC authentication, a user who is a member of a group granted project admin role cannot perform all admin actions because Harbor does not fully resolve group-based role memberships. The fix ensures that when checking user permissions, Harbor considers roles inherited through group membership in addition to directly assigned roles.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "harbor",
+      "graduated",
+      "security",
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "harbor"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "graduated",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/goharbor/harbor/issues/19928",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/goharbor/harbor",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/goharbor/harbor/pull/20409"
+    },
+    "reactions": 9,
+    "comments": 7,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl",
+      "helm"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with harbor installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-13T06:19:41.129Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}
